title: Runtime model-swarm orchestration backend core (durable SwarmCoordinator)
origin: Authored 2026-05-30 under the operator's standing directive (repomem #2395) to build the 'big missing ticket': a RUNTIME local+cloud model swarm/parallel orchestration capability (Master Spec 4.3.9 MULTI_MODEL_PARALLEL + 9 teacher/student distillation + reset-brief 6.3/6.6 run-local-models + operator control room). Research basis: repomem #2396 (Hermes Agent/NousResearch, Claude Code+Agent SDK, PydanticAI, Goose/Block, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen, LangGraph, Dapr Agents) -> 13 Rust/Tokio durable-controller patterns. This MT is the load-bearing BACKEND CORE; wiring/UI/routing/distillation are follow-on MTs (MT-204..).
goal: Build a durable, hardened SwarmCoordinator in handshake_core that spins up and manages MANY independent model sessions (local + cloud) in parallel without weakening governance and without intra-model distributed inference (spec 4.3.9). The coordinator owns the plan, leases, routing bounds, budget, and observability; model creation is injected via a ModelSessionFactory so the same coordinator runs real CandleRuntime/llama.cpp/cloud sessions in production and a real controllable worker in tests.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/swarm_orchestration/ (mod, error, ids, state, breaker, events, factory, coordinator, tests) registered in lib.rs under runtime-full. No app/frontend changes (later waves).
unblock_conditions: MT-202 production model-load flow + ModelRuntime trait exist; ProcessOwnershipLedger, SessionBroker, HbrSwarmLoopCounter, flight_recorder exist.
implementation_notes: HARDENING INVARIANTS (all implemented, no TODOs) from the researched patterns: (A) two-level fan-out bound = tokio::sync::Semaphore concurrency cap + monotonic AtomicU64 lifetime spawn ceiling (default HBR_SWARM_002_LOOP_CAP) with fetch_add reserve + rollback on every rejection path; over-bound returns a typed error, never silent. (B) claim-lease+TTL + single background reaper task that cancels+TEARS DOWN (frees the model via ModelSessionFactory teardown seam, NOT just cancel) + records ledger STOP, with a per-instance bounded respawn counter (no storms). (C) failure-fingerprint circuit breaker keyed on ERROR IDENTITY (sha256 of error class + char-boundary-truncated detail), with an ADMISSION GATE before permit+factory (per-instance last-signature tracked) so an Open signature is suppressed BEFORE paying the load; capacity refusals excluded; record_success heals; cooldown/half-open; settled signatures pruned. (D) atomic check-and-insert (try_insert_ready holds the registry lock across check+insert) so concurrent same-instance spawns yield exactly one Ready + DuplicateInstance with NO orphan ledger START. (E) budget-as-data RunBudget (max_concurrent, max_lifetime_spawns, optional token/cost ceilings) with record_usage decrement seam. (F) ONE spawn path + session registry (one session per ModelInstanceId, idle/terminal eviction, permit released on handle drop); coordinator owns ledger START/STOP symmetry. (G) no std Mutex guard held across .await (clippy await_holding_lock clean); no lock-ordering deadlock. (H) observable lifecycle via SwarmEventSink trait -> FlightRecorder; FR-EVT-SWARM-* kept as a self-contained SwarmFrEventId table (the canonical FrEventId enum is .GOV-locked with a CI alignment test; folding SWARM ids into the .GOV registry is a governance follow-on, MT-204b). FOLLOW-ON WIRING (separate MTs): MT-204 ModelRuntimeState multi-instance + production ModelSessionFactory (real CandleRuntime load + record_spawn + teardown=detach/drop); MT-205 Tauri swarm commands + SwarmControlRoom UI + operator chat box + model spin-up (local+cloud); MT-206 local-vs-cloud routing policy (classifier->tier->escalation + per-provider breaker); MT-207 teacher/student distillation parallel (spec 9) on the coordinator; MT-204b fold FR-EVT-SWARM-* into the .GOV FR registry.
risk_if_missed: Without a durable hardened coordinator, the product cannot run local models for smaller tasks nor swarm local+cloud models in parallel (the WP's core intent + spec 4.3.9 + distillation), and any ad-hoc parallelism risks orphan processes, runaway loops, model leaks, and fleet-wide retry budget burn.
validator_focus: Spec-Realism: the coordinator does REAL orchestration; tests use a real ModelRuntime-implementing controllable worker (not a result-faking mock). Verify the 8 adversarial-review defects (D1 unload/teardown actually frees the model; D2 atomic dedup no-orphan; D3 breaker gates the factory; C4 breaker heals; C5 budget+map pruning; C6 cancel event ordering; C7 START/STOP symmetry) are each closed with a test that fails without the fix. No mocks/placeholders. .GOV FR registry untouched.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Existing committed implementation evidence is present and a KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT PASS was emitted during governance repair; validator review remains pending.

implementation_evidence.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER-20260530-054956 (delegated build sub-agent af1a4aa80edf5a4db + fix sub-agent aa8ef56e9acce8283, KB-reviewed)
implementation_evidence.adversarial_review: sub-agent a38aa3fb8e0febeae found 3 confirmed defects (D1 unload-leak, D2 dedup-orphan, D3 breaker-not-gating) + concerns; all fixed and re-proven (each fix negative-checked: fails without fix, passes with it).
implementation_evidence.proof: cargo test -p handshake_core --lib swarm_orchestration: 25 passed/0 failed (incl 7 orchestration-invariant proofs + 6 defect-fix proofs). cargo check --lib default + --features candle-runtime-engine: clean. fr_event_registry_tests: 17 passed (.GOV registry intact).
implementation_evidence.readiness_note: Governance repair transitioned MT-203 from CLAIMED to READY_FOR_VALIDATION on 2026-06-01 after emitting KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT PASS. This is Kernel Builder handoff readiness only; Integration Validator verdict remains PENDING. Follow-on MTs 204-207 carry the production factory + wiring + UI + routing + distillation.

minimum_controls: Coordinator uses a REAL ModelRuntime-implementing worker in tests (no result-faking mock); peak concurrency measured under real multi-thread JoinSet.
terminate AND lease-expiry actually invoke teardown that frees the model (D1) -- proven a model id is unloaded, not just cancelled.
Concurrent same-instance spawn yields no orphan ledger START (D2) -- START count == STOP count under race.
Open breaker suppresses BEFORE factory.create (D3) -- factory create-call counter does not increment while suppressed.
No std Mutex guard across .await; no unbounded map growth (breaker + respawn maps pruned).
